events.title: Timur defeats Bayezid at Ankara
events.description: Timur (Tamerlane) defeats Ottoman Sultan Bayezid I at Ankara, temporarily halting Ottoman expansion and causing a period of interregnum.

events.title: Early life of Zheng He (voyages precursor)
events.description: Zheng He, later a Ming admiral, rises through Ming service; his voyages (1405–1433) will project Chinese maritime power across the Indian Ocean.

events.title: Battle of Agincourt
events.description: English forces under Henry V win a decisive victory over the French during the Hundred Years' War, noted for longbowmen effectiveness.

events.title: Joan of Arc leads French resurgence
events.description: Joan of Arc inspires French forces, lifts the siege of Orléans, and helps Charles VII's coronation before her capture and execution.

events.title: Zheng He's first voyage begins
events.description: Ming treasure fleets under Admiral Zheng He undertake seven major voyages across the Indian Ocean, reaching Southeast Asia, India, Arabia, and East Africa.

events.title: Early Renaissance cultural flowering
events.description: Art, humanism, and learning flourish in Italy and gradually across Europe, with figures like Donatello, Brunelleschi, and early Renaissance patrons.

events.title: Fall of Constantinople
events.description: Ottoman forces capture Constantinople under Mehmed II, ending the Byzantine Empire and transforming trade and geopolitics between Europe and Asia.

events.title: Printing press spreads in Europe
events.description: Movable type printing spreads from Mainz across Europe, accelerating dissemination of books, ideas, and literacy.

events.title: Vijayanagara Empire reaches cultural and economic peak
events.description: Vijayanagara consolidates power in South India, fostering temple architecture, trade, and resistance to northern incursions.

events.title: Aztec Triple Alliance consolidates power
events.description: The Triple Alliance (Tenochtitlan, Texcoco, Tlacopan) consolidates control over central Mexico, expanding tribute networks and urban development.

events.title: End of Zheng He voyages and Ming maritime retrenchment
events.description: After Zheng He's voyages, Ming court policies shift toward inland focus and maritime expeditions cease, affecting Indian Ocean dynamics.

events.title: Ming internal consolidation and cultural growth
events.description: Ming dynasty consolidates administration, supports arts and commerce, and rebuilds infrastructure after earlier campaigns.

events.title: Portuguese maritime expansion begins along African coast
events.description: Portugal explores the West African coast, establishes trading posts, and develops maritime routes that will lead to rounding Africa and sea routes to Asia.

events.title: Columbus reaches the Americas
events.description: Christopher Columbus lands in the Caribbean under Spanish patronage, initiating sustained contact between Europe and the Americas.

events.title: Treaty of Tordesillas
events.description: Spain and Portugal, with papal approval, divide newly discovered lands outside Europe along a meridian, shaping early colonial claims.

events.title: Vasco da Gama reaches India by sea
events.description: Portuguese navigator Vasco da Gama sails around Africa to reach Calicut, opening a sea route between Europe and India.
